title: Thrymskviða
stanzas: Once Thrym, the giant, in mighty mood,
Had stolen away Mjolnir, the hammer great,
And hid it deep in the earth below,
Eight leagues or more from the sight of men.
Thor came forth in his terrible wrath,
To Loki he spoke with anger dire,
'Go forth and find where my hammer lies,
And bring it back to my hand once more.'
Then Loki went to the land of giants,
And found where Thrym sat proud and high,
'Why dost thou steal from the gods,' said he,
'The hammer that Odin has wrought in aid?'
'I have hidden the hammer deep and far,
Where no man's eye shall ever see,
If thou bring me Freya as my bride,
Then I shall give back the hammer great.'
When Loki told of the giant's demand,
Thor was filled with a fury hot,
But Freya refused to go to the north,
To be wed to the giant so rough and base.
Then Heimdall spoke with wisdom old,
'Thy hammer lies in the giant's keep,
We must dress thee up as a maiden fair,
And send thee to be the giant's bride.'
Though Thor objected with anger fierce,
He donned the dress of a maiden fine,
And Loki dressed him as bridesmaid sweet,
And off they went to the giant's hall.
When Thrym beheld what he thought was his bride,
He called for great feasts to honor the day,
He offered gifts of gold and rings,
As tokens of love to his maiden fair.
But Thor ate and drank with a mighty power,
Eight salmon whole and all the meat,
And drank three barrels of honey-mead,
Till the giant grew struck with wonder great.
'Why doth my bride eat and drink so much?'
Asked Thrym in puzzlement and awe,
'I have not eaten for eight whole days,
So fierce was my longing to come to thee.'
Then Loki spoke with cunning words,
'The bride hath fasted for all these nights,
So eager was she to come to thy hall,
And to be bound to thee with vows of love.'
When the wedding feast was at its height,
Thrym brought forth the hammer great,
He laid it upon the maiden's lap,
To bless and hallow the marriage bond.
But Thor seized the hammer with mighty hand,
And threw off the dress and veil,
He raised Mjolnir high in the air,
And smote the giant with fatal blow.
Then Thor struck down all the giants there,
The old and young, both great and small,
And Thrym was the first to fall and die,
Beneath the hammer's mighty weight.
Then Thor returned to the gods once more,
With Mjolnir recovered and safe in hand,
And all of Asgard made merry rejoice,
That the hammer was back where it should remain.
